natural: 'You idiot / dummy'; a common mild insult. 'đồ' = 'you (thing)' + an adjective; also 'đồ ngốc' (softer), 'đồ điên' (you're crazy).
usageContext: scolding someone; can be affectionate between close friends but insulting to strangers.
warningNotes: Mild but still an insult; fine when teasing a close friend, rude to a stranger.

examples.text: Đồ ngu, sao lại làm thế!
examples.translation: You idiot, why did you do that!

natural: Calling a person 'chó' (dog) is a real insult meaning 'scum / lowlife'. Compounds like 'đồ chó', 'chó chết' (damn / literally 'dead dog') intensify it.
usageContext: cursing at someone you despise; strongly offensive, not casual.
warningNotes: A genuine insult to a person's character; avoid using, but recognize it in fights and cursing.

natural: Crude 'not / no f---ing way'; a vulgar replacement for 'không'. 'đếch' is a shade softer than 'đéo'. Extremely common in rough casual male speech but still coarse.
whoUses: mostly men and young people in rough casual settings
usageContext: emphatic refusal or negation among close friends; taboo in polite or formal company.
warningNotes: Very common in casual male speech but genuinely vulgar; never use with strangers, elders, or in any formal setting.

natural: Vulgar intensifier - 'holy sh--, damn, so f---ing much'. Originally crude, now a super-common exclamation ('vãi!', 'vãi chưởng', 'sợ vãi'). Its uncensored full forms are far stronger (see 'vãi lồn').
usageContext: reacting to something shocking or extreme; casual only.
warningNotes: The bare word 'vãi' is now near-slang, but its origin is vulgar and its full forms are obscene; keep it out of polite speech.

natural: 'Whore / slut'; a strong sexual insult aimed at women. Compounds: 'con đĩ', 'đĩ điếm'. Deeply offensive and misogynistic.
whoUses: used as a heavy insult, mostly in serious conflict
usageContext: vicious cursing; never acceptable in normal conversation.
warningNotes: A severe, degrading insult toward women. Recognize it, never use it.

examples.text: Đừng có gọi người ta là con đĩ.

natural: 'Despicable bastard / scumbag'; condemns someone as morally rotten. 'đồ khốn', 'thằng khốn nạn' are common forms.
usageContext: denouncing someone for a cruel or immoral act.
warningNotes: A strong moral condemnation; avoid using but recognize it.

examples.text: Thằng khốn nạn đó bỏ rơi cả gia đình.
examples.translation: That bastard abandoned his whole family.

natural: Vulgar word for the penis, used both literally and as a crude exclamation/negation ('biết cái cặc' = 'know f--- all'). Very coarse.
usageContext: aggressive or crude talk; heavily taboo in polite company.
warningNotes: Obscene anatomical swear; recognize it in cursing, never use it yourself.

examples.text: Nó biết cái cặc gì đâu.
examples.translation: He knows f--- all about it.

natural: The all-purpose heavy curse - 'f--- / f--- you / goddammit'. Abbreviated 'đm' or 'dcm' (địt con mẹ) in text. Northern equivalent of the strongest everyday swearing.
whoUses: common in angry or very casual rough speech, mostly men
usageContext: venting rage or crude emphasis; extremely offensive to say to someone directly.
warningNotes: One of the strongest common curses; insults the listener's mother. Recognize (including the texting abbreviations), never use.

natural: The full obscene form of the 'vãi' intensifier - 'f---ing hell / holy sh--'. Written 'vl' or 'vcl' (vãi cả lồn) online. Far stronger than bare 'vãi'.
whoUses: young people online and in rough casual speech
usageContext: extreme emphasis or reaction; obscene, casual-only.
warningNotes: Obscene; the 'vl'/'vcl' abbreviations flood social media - recognize them, but do not use in speech.

natural: The most taboo vulgar anatomical word in Vietnamese - equivalent to the strongest English obscenity for female genitalia. Appears in many heavy curses ('cái lồn', 'như lồn').
usageContext: extreme profanity in rage or crude insult.
warningNotes: The single most vulgar/obscene word listed here. Recognize it so you understand insults, but never use it under any circumstance.

examples.translation: Is it good? - Good my a-- (f--- no).
